title: Polish expiration wording and make paste upload progress immediate and more professional

requirements.text: Remove the redundant word “left” from the paste expiration UI so the expiry message reads naturally (e.g., “Expires in 9 min”, not “Expires in 9 min left”).

quotes: Remove the left word as we have expire word.

acceptanceCriteria: On PasteViewPage, the expiration label does not contain the word “left”.
Expiration label examples render as “Expires in 9 min”, “Expires in 1 hr 10 min”, “Expires in 2 days 5 hrs” (no trailing/duplicate wording).
No other UI text regresses into awkward phrasing such as “Expires in Less than 1 min left”.

requirements.text: Improve the paste creation UX so upload/progress feedback appears immediately when the user clicks “Create Paste”, without an initial period where only the button is loading and the progress UI appears later.

quotes: When press create paste button, button goes in loading then after some time loading progress bar comes show upload progress. This experience should be more professional.

acceptanceCriteria: When the user clicks “Create Paste”, an in-form status/progress panel becomes visible immediately (within the same tick/frame) and stays visible through upload + final paste creation.
If file reading/preparation time occurs before upload progress callbacks start, the UI shows a clear intermediate status (e.g., “Preparing files…” or equivalent) instead of appearing idle.
The “Create Paste” button remains disabled during the entire operation to prevent duplicate submissions, and the status panel is the primary indicator (not a delayed secondary indicator).

requirements.text: Investigate and improve slow upload performance for file pastes, focusing on reducing time-to-first-progress and overall upload time where possible, without changing the 50MB per-file limit behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Time-to-first-user-feedback is improved: users see immediate status feedback on submit even before byte-level upload progress begins.
Time-to-first-upload-progress event is minimized (e.g., by restructuring the client flow to begin progress reporting earlier and avoiding unnecessary blocking work before starting the upload).
Upload still enforces the existing 50MB per-file limit and errors remain user-friendly in English.
No changes require additional backend canisters or external services; the system remains single-actor on the backend.

constraints: Do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui (compose existing shadcn components instead).
Do not modify immutable hook files: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx.
Backend must remain a single Motoko actor (all logic in backend/main.mo); only add backend/migration.mo if a stable-state upgrade is required.
nonGoals: Adding new authentication providers (only Internet Identity is supported).
Introducing external databases or third-party storage services.
